zone_6b_notes: Apple trees thrive in Zone 6b â€” Missouri has excellent apple-growing potential with adequate chill hours (800â€“1200 hours below 45Â°F). Plant dormant bare-root trees in early spring (March). Nearly all apples require cross-pollination from a different variety flowering simultaneously. Semi-dwarf rootstocks (M.7, M.106) are best for home gardens (12â€“15 ft). Enterprise, Freedom, and Liberty are disease-resistant modern varieties suited to Missouri's fungal pressure. Jonagold, Gala, and Honeycrisp are flavor standards but require more spray programs. Fire blight is the biggest Missouri threat â€” prune in dry weather and disinfect tools between cuts.

planting.best_planting_time: Plant dormant bare-root trees March to early April
